text.data-narration: Queues are linear data structures that follow the FIFO principle.

g.text: Queues are linear data structures that follow the FIFO principle.

text.data-narration: A queue is a collection of elements that are ordered in a particular way, and elements are added or removed from the queue in a specific order.

g.text: A queue is a collection of elements that are ordered in a particular way, and elements are added or removed from the queue in a specific order.

text.data-narration: Enqueue adds an element to the rear of the queue. Dequeue removes an element from the front of the queue. Peek returns the element at the front of the queue without removing it.

g.text: Enqueue adds an element to the rear of the queue. Dequeue removes an element from the front of the queue. Peek returns the element at the front of the queue without removing it.

text.data-narration: IsEmpty checks if the queue is empty.

g.text: IsEmpty checks if the queue is empty.

text.data-narration: The array has two pointers, front and rear, to keep track of the front and rear elements.

g.text: The array has two pointers, front and rear, to keep track of the front and rear elements.

text.data-narration: A queue can be implemented using an array. The array has two pointers, front and rear, to keep track of the front and rear elements.

g.text: A queue can be implemented using an array. The array has two pointers, front and rear, to keep track of the front and rear elements.
